---
title: "16 Drift Detection Standard"
author: "UIAO Modernization Program"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
format:
  html: default
  docx: default
  pdf: default
  gfm: default
---

# UIAO Drift Detection & Remediation Standard

**Document:** 16
**Phase:** 5 — Data Governance Substrate
**Version:** 1.2
**Status:** Draft
**Classification:** Public
**Date:** 2026-05-11
**Promotes:** PHASE5_RuntimeDriftModel.md (see that document for operational runtime detail)

---

## 1. Purpose

This document formalizes the UIAO Drift Detection & Remediation Standard — the first federated drift taxonomy with severity classification, remediation contracts, and vendor accountability requirements for federal canonical data systems. It promotes and extends `PHASE5_RuntimeDriftModel.md` into the numbered canon.

---

## 2. Drift Taxonomy

UIAO defines five classes of canonical drift:

| Class | Code | Definition |
|---|---|---|
| Schema Drift | `DRIFT-SCHEMA` | Claim structure deviates from canonical schema version |
| Semantic Drift | `DRIFT-SEMANTIC` | Claim value is structurally valid but semantically stale or inconsistent |
| Provenance Drift | `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` | Provenance envelope is incomplete, invalid, or chain is broken |
| Authorization Drift | `DRIFT-AUTHZ` | Claim is transmitted outside its authorized consent envelope |
| Identity Drift | `DRIFT-IDENTITY` | Claim issuer cannot be resolved to a verified identity plane object |

---

## 3. Severity Model

| Severity | Code | Definition | Auto-Remediate | SLA |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Critical | `P1` | Claim in active use; provenance or identity drift | No — halt and alert | Immediate |
| High | `P2` | Schema or semantic drift in a live claim | Yes, if deterministic | 1 hour |
| Medium | `P3` | Drift in a dormant or historical claim | Yes | 24 hours |
| Low | `P4` | Style or metadata drift; no semantic impact | Yes | 72 hours |

---

## 4. Remediation Contract

Every adapter MUST implement the following remediation contract:

```yaml
remediation_contract:
  drift_class: "{DRIFT-*}"
  severity: "{P1|P2|P3|P4}"
  detection_timestamp: "{ISO8601}"
  detected_by: "{adapter_id | workflow_id}"
  auto_remediated: true | false
  remediation_action: "halt | fix | flag | log"
  remediation_timestamp: "{ISO8601}"
  remediation_evidence: "{commit_sha or audit_record_id}"
  escalation_path: "{Canon Steward | Architecture Lead | CISO}"
```

---

## 5. Vendor Accountability Requirements

Certified UIAO adapters MUST:

- Report all drift events to the UIAO telemetry plane within 5 minutes of detection
- Maintain a 30-day drift event log accessible to the Canon Steward
- Achieve < 0.1% P1/P2 drift rate over any rolling 30-day window to maintain certification
- Provide remediation evidence for every P1 event within 24 hours

---

## 6. Compliance Mapping

| NIST Control | Drift Standard Requirement |
|---|---|
| CM-3 (Configuration Change Control) | Schema drift detection and remediation |
| SI-7 (Software Integrity) | Provenance and lineage hash verification |
| CA-7 (Continuous Monitoring) | Automated drift detection workflow |
| IR-6 (Incident Reporting) | P1/P2 escalation paths |

---

## 7. Federal SSOT Context

The drift taxonomy formalized in §2 is the substrate mechanism through
which UIAO supports federal SSOT and continuous-monitoring mandates over
the identity layer. Each drift class corresponds to a specific class of
condition under which a federal mandate's assumptions about the
authoritative state of the identity namespace no longer hold.
`DRIFT-IDENTITY` and `DRIFT-AUTHZ` in particular are the two classes most
directly load-bearing for federal identity SSOT, because they surface
the conditions under which a principal's authoritative state can no
longer be verified against canon — which is the precise failure mode the
federal SSOT mandates exist to prevent.

| Federal mandate | Load-bearing drift class(es) | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| Evidence Act — Data Asset Inventories | `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` | Every change to a canon record produces a provenance chain entry; drift findings are themselves inventory artifacts |
| Federal Data Strategy — SSOT Designation | `DRIFT-IDENTITY`, `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` | Identity namespace as root SSOT; drift findings are the condition under which the SSOT designation is no longer trustworthy |
| OMB M-22-09 (Federal Zero Trust) / EO 14028 | `DRIFT-IDENTITY`, `DRIFT-AUTHZ` | Continuous identity verification; authorization-state drift surfaces the condition under which a Zero Trust policy decision is no longer load-bearing |
| FedRAMP 20x continuous monitoring | All five classes | Drift findings accumulate continuously and are emitted as Evidence Bundle artifacts rather than assembled at assessment time |
| NIST 800-63 AAL requirements | `DRIFT-IDENTITY` | Authenticator assurance level encoded per principal; drift surfaces conditions under which AAL is no longer continuously verified |
| DAMA-DMBOK — Quality, Lineage, Metadata | `DRIFT-SCHEMA`, `DRIFT-SEMANTIC`, `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` | Drift classes are the operational form of DAMA-DMBOK data-quality and lineage concerns applied to the identity layer |
| CDO Data Governance Board requirements | `DRIFT-AUTHZ`, `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` | Ownership, accountability, and access-decision drift; findings are the artifact a Data Governance Board consumes |

For the federal mandate alignment that frames this cross-walk — including
the COR schema as identity MDM hub, the provenance chain as data lineage
equivalent, and the six control planes as federated governance — see the
[Federal SSOT Alignment whitepaper](../customer-documents/whitepapers/federal-ssot-alignment.qmd).

For the structural compliance conflict the GCC-Moderate boundary creates
against three of these mandates simultaneously (TIC 3.0 × Zero Trust ×
FedRAMP 20x) and the Evidence Bundle finding shape the substrate emits
for it, see [B.1.1 The Three-Way GCC-Moderate Compliance Conflict](../customer-documents/compliance/boundary-authorization/B1-1-gcc-moderate-three-way-conflict.qmd).

For the identity-substrate role beneath the Microsoft Fabric + OneLake +
Purview Azure SSOT stack — including the discovery → OrgPath enrichment
→ Purview registration → Fabric Domain assignment → drift verification
loop — see [OrgPath Narrative 07a — UIAO Beneath the Azure SSOT Stack](../customer-documents/orgpath-narrative/07a-uiao-beneath-the-azure-ssot-stack.qmd).

### 7.1 SPN Drift Detection (`MSSQLSvc/*`)

Service Principal Names registered in Active Directory under the
`MSSQLSvc/*` prefix (and the related service-class prefixes captured by
the `spn_inventory` artifact emitted by the AD survey adapter) are the
canonical example of a `DRIFT-IDENTITY` failure mode that surfaces only
after a migration event has completed. When a SQL Server service account
is migrated from AD to an Entra ID managed identity, a different domain
principal, or a group Managed Service Account, the SPN registration must
be explicitly re-established against the new principal. If it is not,
Kerberos authentication to that SQL instance breaks silently — LDAP-
dependent applications lose connectivity, linked-server connections
fail, and Reporting Services delegation chains disintegrate. None of
those failures appears immediately, which is the precise reason the
federal SSOT mandates require continuous identity-state verification
rather than point-in-time attestation.

The substrate's response is structured. The `spn_inventory` artifact
defined in
[`src/uiao/schemas/orgtree-readiness/orgtree-readiness.schema.json`]({{< meta canon-reference >}}#spnInventory)
(introduced in PR #395) captures the pre-migration SPN registration
state with phase-tagged provenance. The post-migration state is captured
in the same artifact form on the migrated tenant, and the drift
comparison emits findings in four shapes:

| Drift condition | Drift class | Severity | Detection signal |
|---|---|---|---|
| SPN present pre-migration, absent post-migration | `DRIFT-IDENTITY` | P1 — Kerberos chain broken | Set-difference: SPNs in pre-migration inventory not in post-migration |
| SPN host or port changed without a recorded migration event | `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` | P2 | Same `(service_class, principal_name)` key, different `host_or_fqdn` or `port_or_instance` |
| SPN owner (service-account principal) changed without provenance | `DRIFT-AUTHZ` | P1 | Same SPN, different `principal_name` / `distinguished_name`, no transition record in provenance chain |
| SPN present in AD but no Entra ID Kerberos delegation configured | `DRIFT-IDENTITY` | P2 — hybrid gap | Cross-reference fails between AD SPN record and Entra workload-identity federation config |

Each drift finding is written to the parent OrgTree-readiness Evidence
Bundle through the existing `findings` array, with the `spn_record`
identifying the SPN that triggered the finding (via the
`drift_finding_ref` back-pointer on the SPN record). This closes the
round-trip established by the
[Federal SSOT Alignment whitepaper §3.3](../customer-documents/whitepapers/federal-ssot-alignment.qmd)
between the drift engine and the federal continuous-monitoring mandate:
the SPN inventory is the pre-migration baseline, the post-migration
inventory is the verification artifact, and the drift findings are the
auditor-consumable evidence that the substrate has detected the failure
mode rather than letting it surface as a P1 incident weeks later.

Operationally, the four drift conditions above map to the standard
remediation contract in §4. The P1 conditions halt-and-alert per the
severity model (§3); the P2 conditions auto-remediate when deterministic
(re-registering an SPN against the migrated principal is deterministic;
attributing an unknown owner is not).

---

## 8. Cross-References

- `docs/PHASE5_RuntimeDriftModel.md` — Operational runtime drift model (promoted by this document)
- `docs/15_ProvenanceProfile.md` — Provenance envelope that drift detection validates
- `docs/17_ConsentEnvelope.md` — Authorization drift detection
- `docs/18_ClaimCatalog.md` — Schema drift detection against catalog versions
- `scripts/detect_drift.py` — Drift detection automation
- `docs/customer-documents/whitepapers/federal-ssot-alignment.qmd` — Federal mandate alignment over the identity layer
- `docs/customer-documents/compliance/boundary-authorization/B1-1-gcc-moderate-three-way-conflict.qmd` — TIC 3.0 × Zero Trust × FedRAMP 20x structural compliance conflict
- `docs/customer-documents/orgpath-narrative/07a-uiao-beneath-the-azure-ssot-stack.qmd` — Identity-substrate role beneath the Azure SSOT stack

---

## 9. Revision History

| Version | Date | Author | Summary |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1.0 | 2026-04-01 | UIAO Canon Engine | Initial canonical release — promotes PHASE5_RuntimeDriftModel.md |
| 1.1 | 2026-05-11 | UIAO Canon Engine | Adds §7 Federal SSOT Context — drift-class-to-mandate cross-walk + cross-refs to federal-ssot-alignment whitepaper, B.1.1 three-way conflict finding, and OrgPath Narrative chapter 07a. Renumbers prior §7 Cross-References → §8, §8 Revision History → §9. External references to §2 and §4 are unaffected. |
| 1.2 | 2026-05-11 | UIAO Canon Engine | Adds §7.1 SPN Drift Detection (`MSSQLSvc/*`) — concrete drift-condition table tied to the `spn_inventory` artifact introduced in PR #395. No section renumbering. |
